title: Kundalini Awakening vs Third Eye Opening
meta_description: Hey, you. Yeah, you, staring at your screen at 3 AM, heart thumping, mind racing, wondering if you’re losing your damn mind. I’ve been there. I know that f
body:
Kundalini Awakening vs Third Eye Opening

Hey, you. Yeah, you, staring at your screen at 3 AM, heart thumping, mind racing, wondering if you’re losing your damn mind. I’ve been there. I know that feeling of being utterly alone, convinced you’ve stumbled into something terrifying and irreversible. You’re probably trying to figure out what the hell is happening to you, right? Is it Kundalini? Is it your Third Eye? Or is it just a full-blown breakdown? Let’s talk about it, because honestly, there’s a lot of confusing crap out there, and you don’t need more of that right now.

First off, breathe. Just for a second. You’re still here. You’re reading this. That’s a start. I’m not gonna sugarcoat it, but I’m also not gonna scare you more than you already are. What you’re experiencing, whatever it is, it’s real. It’s not in your head in the way people usually mean it. Your body, your mind, your spirit - they’re going through something. And while it feels like you're the only one, you're not. I promise you that.

So, Kundalini awakening versus Third Eye opening. They get thrown around a lot, often interchangeably, and that’s part of the problem. It adds to the confusion when you’re already drowning. Let’s break it down, simply, directly. Because you need clarity, not more woo-woo jargon.

What Even IS Kundalini?

Look, Kundalini. It’s not some fluffy New Age concept, even though it’s been co-opted that way. It’s an ancient, ancient concept, deeply rooted in spiritual traditions, particularly from India. Imagine it as a dormant energy, coiled at the base of your spine. Like a sleeping serpent, that’s how they describe it. And when it wakes up, it’s not always a gentle stretch and a yawn. No, it can be a seismic event. Know what I mean?

When this energy awakens, it’s supposed to rise up through your central energy channel, your sushumna, piercing through the chakras - those energy centers along your spine. The idea is that as it rises, it purifies, it transforms, it expands your consciousness. Sounds great on paper, right? Like some spiritual superpower. But the reality, for many, myself included, is far from serene.

I’ve seen this described as a blissful journey. And sure, for some, maybe it is. But for many, it’s a goddamn nightmare. It’s intense heat, uncontrollable shaking, visions that feel more like hallucinations, sounds that aren’t there, sensations that defy explanation. It’s like your nervous system gets rewired while you’re still trying to live your life. You feel like you’re short-circuiting. You might feel immense pressure in your head, strange tingling, electric shocks. Your body feels like it's vibrating constantly. Sleep becomes a luxury, food becomes a challenge, and just existing becomes an Olympic sport.

Here’s the thing: Kundalini isn't just a feeling in your head. It’s physical. It’s visceral. It affects every cell in your body. It can trigger old traumas, bring up suppressed emotions with a vengeance. You might feel incredible bliss one moment, then plunge into despair the next. It’s a roller coaster, but one where the tracks are constantly being built and torn down around you.

If you're looking for something that really captures the raw experience, I'd suggest checking out The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link). It’s an older book, but it’s got some solid accounts that might make you feel less alone in your weirdness. Or The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) is another good one. Bonnie Greenwell knows her stuff; she’s been working with people like us for a long time.

The "Crisis" Part

What I went through, and what many others go through, isn't just an "awakening." It's a spiritual emergency, or as some call it, a Kundalini crisis. You're not having a bad trip; your entire operating system is being upgraded without your permission, and it's crashing repeatedly. You might feel disconnected from reality, from your body, from other people. You might feel like you're losing your sanity. And honestly, it feels a lot like it. That's why it's so terrifying. You're questioning everything you thought you knew about yourself and the world.

The system is trying to integrate this massive influx of energy, and if your body, mind, and nervous system aren't ready - which, let's be real, most aren't in our modern world - it can be incredibly destabilizing. It’s not just a spiritual event; it’s a psychological and physiological upheaval. It’s messy. It’s disorienting. It’s not something you can just meditate away. Trust me, I tried.

And What About the Third Eye?

Okay, so now the Third Eye. This is often talked about as if it’s a separate, distinct thing from Kundalini. And in some ways, it is, but it’s also deeply intertwined. The Third Eye, or Ajna chakra, is located between your eyebrows. It’s often associated with intuition, psychic abilities, inner vision, clarity, and higher perception. When people talk about "opening" their Third Eye, they’re usually talking about developing these capacities.

An "opening" of the Third Eye can manifest as increased intuition, vivid dreams, seeing auras, having premonitions, or just a general sense of knowing things you shouldn’t. You might feel a pressure or tingling sensation in the center of your forehead. You might start seeing flashes of light, geometric patterns, or even full-blown visions. For some, it’s a gradual unfolding, a gentle increase in sensitivity. For others, it can be quite sudden and intense.

The key distinction here is that while Kundalini is a massive, full-body energy phenomenon, the Third Eye is more specific to perception and intuition. Think of Kundalini as the entire power grid coming online, and the Third Eye as one specific, highly sensitive sensor on that grid becoming active. Does that land?

The Overlap and the Confusion

Here’s where it gets confusing: a Kundalini awakening often *includes* a Third Eye opening. As Kundalini rises, it's supposed to activate all the chakras, and that includes the Ajna chakra. So, if you're having a full-blown Kundalini experience, you're likely also experiencing Third Eye phenomena. But you can also have aspects of a Third Eye opening without the full, overwhelming force of Kundalini. You could be developing your intuition, having vivid dreams, or seeing things without your entire nervous system going haywire.

The problem is, if you’re suddenly seeing things, hearing things, and feeling immense pressure in your head, it’s easy to just label it "Third Eye opening" and think it’s a cool spiritual superpower. But if it’s accompanied by extreme heat, shaking, emotional purging, and a feeling of impending doom - that’s probably more than just your Third Eye. That’s the whole damn system. That’s Kundalini, my friend.

I’ve seen people chase Third Eye experiences with intense meditation or specific practices, not realizing they’re poking a sleeping giant that could wake up the whole show. And when the whole show wakes up without proper preparation, without grounding, without understanding, that’s when things get rough. That's when you end up like me, or like you, Googling at 3 AM.

Why Does This Happen?

Honestly, no one knows for sure. It can be triggered by intense spiritual practice - meditation, yoga, breathwork. It can be spontaneous, seemingly out of nowhere. Trauma can play a role, sometimes years later, as the body tries to process and release stored energy. Sometimes it’s just a natural unfolding, a spontaneous evolution of consciousness that our modern lives aren't equipped to handle. It can also be influenced by psychedelic experiences, which can kick-start these energies in unexpected ways.

It’s not just "spiritual people" who experience this. I’ve talked to engineers, stay-at-home parents, artists - people from all walks of life. It doesn’t discriminate. And it doesn’t care if you believe in chakras or not. Once it starts, it starts.

The truth is, our culture doesn't have a good container for these experiences. We don't have shamans or village elders who understand what's happening. We have doctors who might diagnose you with anxiety, psychosis, or depression - and while those things can certainly be present, they don't always explain the full picture. That’s why you feel so lost. There’s no clear path, no instruction manual.

If you're interested in the broader context of spiritual emergencies, not just Kundalini, When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) is a really good resource. And The Stormy Search for the Self by Stanislav & Christina Grof (paid link) is a classic that covers all sorts of non-ordinary states of consciousness. These books can help you understand that you're not an anomaly; you're part of a phenomenon that's been documented for centuries, just not in our everyday medical texts.

What Do You Do Now?

Okay, so you’ve got a better idea of the difference. What now? You're probably still feeling like crap, right? Here’s what I learned, the hard way:

  • Grounding is everything. Forget trying to open more chakras or having more visions. Your system is overloaded. You need to pull that energy DOWN. Think physical: walking in nature, eating nourishing food, spending time with animals, doing simple, repetitive tasks. Get your hands dirty. Connect to the earth.
  • Simplify your life. Seriously. Cut out anything that adds stress. Reduce sensory input. Don’t chase more spiritual experiences. You’ve had enough for a while.
  • Find support. This is crucial. You need someone who understands, or at least believes you. It might be hard to find in your immediate circle. Look for spiritual emergence coaches, therapists who work with non-ordinary states, or support groups online. Just having someone listen without judgment can be a lifeline.
  • Don’t stop living. It’s tempting to withdraw completely, to hide from the world. And sometimes you need that. But try to maintain some semblance of routine. Go to work if you can. See a friend, even if it’s just for a coffee. This helps keep you connected to consensus reality, which is vital when your inner world is so chaotic.
  • Listen to your body. It’s trying to tell you something. Rest when you need to rest. Eat what feels right. Move your body gently. Don’t force anything.
  • Be patient. This isn’t a quick fix. It’s a process. It can take months, even years, to integrate these energies. There will be good days and bad days. Don’t beat yourself up for the bad days. Just keep showing up.
  • Journal. Get it all out of your head and onto paper. The fear, the visions, the sensations. It helps to externalize it, to see it laid out. It can help you make sense of the chaos, or at least track patterns.

You’re not going crazy. You’re going through something incredibly intense, something that humanity has grappled with for millennia. It's a natural, albeit often painful, aspect of human consciousness. It feels like a curse right now, I know. But if you can get through it, if you can integrate it, it will change you in ways you can’t even imagine. It will make you stronger, more compassionate, more real. You’ll see the world with new eyes, not just your Third Eye, but your actual eyes, with a depth you never knew existed.

So yeah, it’s scary. It’s isolating. But you’re not alone in it. And you will get through this. Just keep breathing. Keep grounding. Keep seeking support. One foot in front of the other. That’s all you can do right now. And that’s enough.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
